body.title: Exclude WORD_GRAPH nodes from hub-spoke sync + fix accumulate_unresolved race
body.result: WORD_GRAPH portable=0 via m063 migration + INSERT fix; accumulate_unresolved uses atomic UPDATE+RETURNING (button pattern)
body.intention: I intend that hub-spoke sync is not stressed by 57K scaffolding nodes and that watchlist counts are race-free.
body.description:
Expert audit (Distributed Systems): (1) 57K WORD_GRAPH nodes are scope='class' so they flood the hub-spoke sync (57K upserts per sync cycle); mark them portable=0 or add sync exclusion. (2) accumulate_unresolved() does read-modify-write without FOR UPDATE — concurrent stores can lose count increments. Fix: use UPDATE SET watch_edge_count = watch_edge_count + 1 atomic SQL.

**Affected files:** devices/igor/memory/cortex.py (sync exclusion + accumulate_unresolved fix)
**Design rules:** HIGH-inertia cortex.py — Akien pre-approval required before coding
**Scope boundary:** IN — sync exclusion for WORD_GRAPH, atomic increment for accumulate_unresolved; OUT — sync protocol changes
**Completion criteria:** PROC_MEMORY_SYNC does not attempt to upsert WORD_GRAPH nodes; accumulate_unresolved uses atomic UPDATE SET x = x + 1
